name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Arabian Sea / Gulf of Oman — ~124 nm off southern Iran (BBC satellite imagery Apr 11-12) — forward-most CSG for Epic Fury blockade enforcement
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — blockade enforcement and Hormuz mine clearance support
notes: Day 48: Lincoln remains forward-most CSG for Epic Fury — ~124 nm off southern Iran in the Arabian Sea / Gulf of Oman per BBC satellite imagery (Apr 11-12). CSG-3 with CVW-9 embarked; Air Defense Commander USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121); DESRON 21 including USS Spruance (DDG-111). USS Spruance (DDG-111) turned back an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Gulf of Oman Apr 14 (Stars & Stripes, Apr 16) — 10th interception. Blockade Day 4 CENTCOM tally: 13-14 vessels turned back in 72 hours; no breaches claimed, but BBC Verify/MarineTraffic disputes CENTCOM's 'zero penetrations' claim with at least 4 Iran-related transits Apr 14. 20+ non-Iran-bound commercial ships passed freely. CENTCOM reports >10,000 personnel enforcing blockade with 'more than a dozen warships and dozens of aircraft.' Mine clearance via USS Pioneer and USS Chief dispatched from Singapore. USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 13: 291 total Battle Force ships (232 USS, 59 USNS), 105 deployed, 86 underway. IRGC Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi declared Apr 15 a three-sea counter-blockade doctrine (Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea). Mojtaba Khamenei 'military adviser' warned Iran would 'sink American ships in the Strait of Hormuz.' No kinetic Iranian action Apr 15-16. Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~216 hours (expires ~Apr 21-22). Hegseth (Apr 16 Pentagon): US 'locked and loaded' to restart combat if talks collapse. WSJ: Trump weighing resumption of limited strikes to break Islamabad stalemate — NOT implemented. UK explicitly told BBC it will not assist the blockade. France/UK building separate ~15-country freedom-of-navigation coalition under UN framework.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, CENTCOM, Stars & Stripes, BBC Verify, Apr 16 reporting

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Eastern Mediterranean — departed Split Apr 2; post-Vietnam deployment record in force
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets (CVW-8), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: Extended Epic Fury deployment — Eastern Mediterranean — post-Vietnam carrier deployment record
notes: Day 48: Ford remains in Eastern Mediterranean supporting Epic Fury and Red Sea/Lebanon coverage; departed Split Apr 2 with post-Vietnam deployment record in force. CVW-8 embarked; DESRON 2 includes USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71). USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (Apr 13): Navy maintains 291 battle force ships (232 USS + 59 USNS), 105 deployed, 86 underway. Three-carrier posture: Lincoln (Arabian Sea forward-most), Ford (Eastern Med), Tripoli ARG (Arabian Sea). No change in tasking Apr 15-16 — Lebanon front continues to demand TACAIR / ISR support. IDF strike on Nabatieh Apr 16 killed 19 incl. 13 Lebanese State Security personnel; Hezbollah Apr 16 struck Ashdod (claimed Naval Base).
source: USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (Apr 13), Apr 16 manifest

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Eastern Mediterranean — entered Med ~Apr 13 via Gibraltar — third carrier reinforcement for Epic Fury
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Third carrier reinforcement for Epic Fury — Eastern Mediterranean — provides 3-CSG posture with Lincoln (Gulf) and Ford (Med)
notes: Day 46: Bush CSG entered the Mediterranean ~April 13 via Gibraltar providing the third carrier reinforcement. CSG-10 with CVW-7 embarked, commanded by RADM Alexis Walker. Escorts: USS Ross (DDG-71), USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Mason (DDG-87). USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 13: 291 total Battle Force ships (232 USS, 59 USNS), 105 deployed, 86 underway. Three-CSG posture now in place for first time since early in war: Lincoln (Arabian Sea/Gulf), Ford (Eastern Med — breaking post-Vietnam record), Bush (entering Eastern Med). Bush arrival dramatically increases combat capacity amid Islamabad collapse and the US naval blockade of Iranian ports.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, SOF News, CBS News, Apr 14 reporting

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: CENTCOM AOR — Arabian Sea — operational with F-35Bs, ~3,500 personnel
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53K King Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 44: Operational in CENTCOM AOR since Mar 27. 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines/sailors) embarked, F-35B ops confirmed. Tripoli operates as 'Lightning Carrier' with ~20 F-35Bs. Combined with 82nd Airborne: ~4,200+ ground troops forward-deployed. Ceasefire on Iran front technically holds ~120 hours but Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12 and Trump declared US naval blockade of Hormuz — Tripoli ARG well-positioned for potential island seizure, mine countermeasures, or blockade support operations.
source: WSJ (Mar 24), USNI Fleet Tracker (Mar 23), USNI News (Mar 28), Reuters

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: Bahrain / Strait of Hormuz — conducting mine countermeasures
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz from Bahrain
notes: Day 37: Three LCS conducting MCM from Bahrain. CRITICAL GAP: Iran possesses estimated 5,000-6,000 naval mines. Last four Avenger-class minesweepers in Bahrain decommissioned Sep 2025. Only 3 LCS with MCM mission packages available; Pentagon OT&E confirmed no operational testing of LCS MCM package in FY2025. Experts estimate clearing Hormuz requires 16 MCM vessels; Navy has 7 total (4 Avengers in Japan). CENTCOM destroyed 44+ minelayers.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, CRS Report, CENTCOM, UK MoD, Bloomberg, Apr 5 reporting

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 23: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Weapons confirmed in use include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms 8,000+ US targets struck cumulative.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 23: Part of expanded ~12-14 destroyer presence in region. USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pinckney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), plus 4 DDGs in Eastern Med. Total: ~12-14 destroyers in region.
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea

name: USS Boxer
strike_group: Boxer Amphibious Ready Group / 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Western Pacific — departed Pearl Harbor Apr 1; transiting western Pacific; arrival in theater estimated late April to early May
aircraft: AV-8B Harrier II or F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Originally scheduled Indo-Pacific; Pentagon indicated may relieve Tripoli's Asia tasking or follow to Middle East
notes: Day 44: Boxer ARG with 11th MEU departed Pearl Harbor Apr 1 and transiting the western Pacific. Boxer carries ~2,500 Marines from Camp Pendleton. Pentagon indicated Boxer would either relieve Tripoli's Asia tasking or follow it to the Middle East. Expected to arrive in CENTCOM area late April (approximately Apr 22-28). Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12 and Trump declared US naval blockade of Hormuz — Boxer arrival would provide additional amphibious capacity for potential island seizure or blockade operations.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon, Apr 12 reporting

name: HMS Anson
mission_summary: UK submarine supporting coalition operations in Arabian Sea
notes: Day 29: Royal Navy Astute-class SSN. Departed Perth March 6. Tomahawk-armed. Northern Arabian Sea (reported). No official UK confirmation of exact position.

name: 82nd Airborne Division
area: CENTCOM AOR — undisclosed Middle East locations — 1st BCT 'Devil Brigade' (~2,000 paratroopers)
mission_summary: Ground combat force — 1st BCT Immediate Response Force forward-deployed; speculated mission sets include Kharg Island seizure or Strait of Hormuz operations
notes: Day 44: ~2,000 paratroopers from 1st BCT (Immediate Response Force) remain forward-deployed to undisclosed Middle East locations since March 24-25. ~50,000+ US service members participated in Operation Epic Fury. Forces remain deployed — no drawdown. Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12 after 21 hours; Trump declared US Navy will blockade Hormuz. 82nd Airborne postured for contingencies if ceasefire collapses after Apr 22 expiration. 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29. Congress returns Apr 14.
source: Reuters, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon, Apr 12 reporting

name: HMS Dragon
notes: Day 29: Type 45 destroyer. Departed Portsmouth March 10 for air defense mission in Eastern Mediterranean.

name: FS Charles de Gaulle
area: Eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay — expanded multinational escort including French, Italian, Spanish, Dutch, German vessels
mission_summary: French carrier operations — Eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay. France NOT participating offensively in Epic Fury but has shot down Iranian drones 'in legitimate defense'
notes: Day 41: Charles de Gaulle remains in the eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay with an expanded multinational escort including French, Italian, Spanish, Dutch, and German vessels. France is NOT participating offensively in Epic Fury but has shot down Iranian drones 'in legitimate defense.' Macron announced 8 additional frigates and 2 Mistral-class amphibious ships deployed to the region on March 9. French leadership has demanded Lebanon's inclusion in the ceasefire — Macron stated Lebanon was 'fully included.'
source: French MoD / Reuters / Macron statement / Apr 9 reporting

name: VMFA-311 Marine F-35Cs
area: RAF Lakenheath, UK — staging en route to CENTCOM
aircraft: F-35C Lightning II (first 5 of 10 arrived Mar 23-24; first-ever land-based F-35C combat deployment)
mission_summary: Marine F-35C squadron staging through UK en route to CENTCOM theater
notes: Day 27: The War Zone confirmed Mar 24 that first 5 of 10 planned F-35Cs from VMFA-311 'Tomcats' arrived RAF Lakenheath Mar 23-24, having departed MCAS Miramar Mar 10. First-ever land-based combat deployment of the F-35C. Wikipedia states all 10 arrived; second batch of 5 UNVERIFIED in primary reporting. Also notable: AC-130J Ghostrider gunship arrived Lakenheath from Iceland Mar 24.
source: The War Zone Mar 24, USNI News, Pentagon
